question: Should users be able to drag-and-drop cards between phase columns to change a feature's phase, or is the Kanban view display-only (navigation via click-to-detail)?
options: Display-only — cards show current phase, clicking navigates to detail page, phase changes only through the pipeline
Drag-and-drop to move features between phases (writes to backend, updates current_phase)
Drag-and-drop with confirmation dialog before persisting the phase change

question: How should the view toggle (list vs Kanban) be persisted across page reloads?
options: Persist in localStorage so it survives reloads and browser restarts
Persist in the URL query string (e.g. ?view=kanban) so it is shareable and bookmarkable
Do not persist — default to list view on every page load, user toggles each session

question: What should the Kanban board show for columns that have no features in them?
options: Always show all six phase columns with an empty-state message (e.g. 'No features') inside
Hide columns that have zero features to save horizontal space
Show all columns but collapse empty ones to a narrow strip that can be expanded

question: Within a phase column, how should cards be ordered?
options: By priority (P1 first, then P2, then P3), ties broken by most recently updated
By most recently updated (descending), regardless of priority
By status first (in_progress, waiting_for_human, gate_blocked, done) then priority

question: How should the board handle horizontal overflow when all six columns don't fit on screen (e.g. mobile, narrow viewports)?
options: Horizontal scroll — columns stay fixed-width, board scrolls left/right
Responsive column wrap — columns reflow into a grid on narrow screens
Horizontal scroll on desktop, stacked vertical list of phases on mobile

question: Should each phase column display a count of features in that column (e.g. 'Inception (3)')?
options: Yes — show feature count in each column header
No — keep headers minimal with just the phase name
